title: Separate Dry vs Wet Strategy Regimes
summary: Recent pit-stop modeling work explicitly drops wet/intermediate laps because wet stops are reactive to weather rather than predictable degradation.
rationale: Recent pit-stop modeling work explicitly drops wet/intermediate laps because wet stops are reactive to weather rather than predictable degradation. The current best only ordinal-encodes wet/intermediate compounds, which may blur two different decision processes.
implementation_hint: Add dry_regime flags: is_dry_compound, is_wet_or_intermediate, dry_compound_age, wet_compound_age, and interactions with LapTime_Delta/RaceProgress. Train either one model with regime interactions or a dry-only model blended with a simple high-variance wet/intermediate model.
expected_effect: Could improve ranking by preventing rare wet/reactive rows from distorting dry pit-window learning while still assigning plausible probabilities to wet rows.
risk: If test contains many wet-like cases and train has enough signal, isolating them may reduce sample size and hurt calibration.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
